key_insight: MXtalGFlow turns molecular crystal generation into sampling a thermodynamically meaningful Boltzmann distribution over crystal structures rather than just listing isolated low-energy candidates.
engineering_relevance: This gives crystal structure prediction a distributional foundation that can help prioritize polymorphs, quantify competing packings, and guide materials screening under realistic energetic conditions.
method: Define a canonical crystal parameterization for target molecules and space groups.
Train energy-based GFlowNets to match the Boltzmann distribution over crystal structures.
Analyze sampled basins around known polymorphs and compare against multiple energy models.
evidence_signal: The framework is evaluated on two molecules under two energy functions (Lennard-Jones and Universal Model for Atoms), recovering known polymorph basins and surfacing additional competitive packing modes.
watch_item: Results depend on the chosen parameterization, space-group constraints, and energy model, so distribution quality may shift if these assumptions are changed.
paper_title: A Distributional Framework for Generative Modeling of Molecular Crystals
